settings.title: Settings
settings.host_helper: Public host name where the timestamp authority is reachable. It must resolve to this server.
settings.transport_only: These settings only concern the HTTPS transport
settings.transport_only_desc: Host name, Let's Encrypt and HTTP-to-HTTPS redirection configure the TLS layer in front of the service. They do NOT affect the RFC 3161 timestamp signing certificate, which is managed on the Certificate page.
settings.lets_encrypt_note_desc: The HTTP-01 challenge requires that ports 80/443 for this FQDN reach this node. If the request arrives via DNAT/forwarding from a gateway, leave Let's Encrypt disabled and run the route with the existing certificate or a wildcard; renewal is handled transparently by the node's Traefik.
settings.domain_already_used_in_traefik: This host name is already used by another instance.

certificate.title: Certificate
certificate.intro_desc: This is the long-lived, self-signed certificate chain the server uses to sign timestamp tokens. It is independent of the HTTPS transport certificate. Clients that verify your tokens must trust the Root CA below.
certificate.no_certificate: No certificate found yet. Save the settings once to generate the initial chain.
certificate.subject: Subject
certificate.selftest_desc: Issue a real timestamp against the local server and verify it against the signing chain (ping + Status: Granted + openssl ts -verify).
certificate.rotate_warning_desc: A new self-signed chain is generated and the old one is archived with a timestamp. Timestamp tokens issued before the rotation stay verifiable only against the archived chain, and clients must trust the new Root CA.
certificate.rotate_confirm_desc: Are you sure you want to archive the current chain and generate a new one? This cannot be undone.
certificate.root_cn_helper: Common name of the new Root CA. Leave as is to keep the current name.
certificate.upload_desc: Install an externally obtained timestamping certificate (e.g. a commercial RFC 3161 certificate). The leaf must carry the critical extendedKeyUsage=timeStamping.
certificate.upload_chain_helper: Leaf certificate first, followed by intermediates and the root. Paste here or pick a file below.
certificate.upload_chain_file: ...or load the chain from a file
certificate.upload_passphrase_helper: Passphrase protecting the private key. Leave empty if the key is unencrypted.
certificate.invalid_chain: The certificate chain could not be parsed.
certificate.missing_timestamping_eku: The leaf certificate does not carry the critical timeStamping extended key usage and cannot be used as an RFC 3161 signer.
certificate.key_mismatch: The private key does not match the leaf certificate.
certificate.invalid_passphrase: The private key could not be decrypted with the supplied passphrase.
certificate.server_rejected_certificate: The timestamp server did not accept the uploaded certificate; the previous chain has been restored.
